TeacherTools Data-ready reporting
Home Pricing FAQ Contact
Local-only beta Open TeacherTools

Quick answers about TeacherTools.

Everything runs locally today. AI features use the Gemini API — no student data is stored server-side.

Where does my data live?

+
It stays in your browser. Nothing leaves your machine unless you export a file or use an AI feature — those send only the minimum required context to the Gemini API and nothing is stored on any server we operate.

What files can I upload?

+
CSV, XLSX, and XLS. You can drag multiple files or load an entire folder. Each file becomes a separate class you can switch between instantly.

Do I need an account?

+
Not yet. Everything works without signing in. Email-based access will be optional when cloud sync arrives — you will never be forced to create an account to use the core features.

Does TeacherTools use AI?

+
Yes — for two features. Report Comments uses the Gemini API to draft and refine per-student comments based on their marks, performance level, and upcoming assignments. The Marking Assistant uses Gemini Vision to read student names and scores from a photo of a handwritten assignment page. In both cases, only the minimum needed data is sent to the API and nothing is logged or retained.

How does the Marking Assistant work?

+
You enter a grade label and assignment name, then scan individual student pages one at a time using your phone camera or a file upload. The AI reads the student's name and their score from the page — typically in about two seconds — and adds a row to an editable table. When you're done, download the whole table as a CSV.

How does report comment generation work?

+
There are two modes. Bulk mode generates a comment for every student in the class at once using their marks and performance level — good for a first draft. Advanced mode lets you fine-tune one student at a time: pick specific assignments to mention, set upcoming or late work, and click "Refine Comment" to adjust any bulk-generated draft. Both modes use the student's final grade to set the tone of the comment.

Can I print formatted reports?

+
Yes. Use Print View to format attendance, marking, and drill sheets before printing or saving as PDF. You can print all three templates for the selected class at once — the app will prompt you to choose marking columns before printing.

How do I get help?

+
Visit the contact page for questions, onboarding, or feature requests. We usually respond within a school day.

Still stuck?

Visit the contact page and tell us what you are trying to do. We usually respond within a school day.

Get help
